Official Mandatory Fertility Program
The National Population Support Initiative (NPSI) is a federally administered program created to address the country’s declining birthrate by encouraging stable reproductive partnerships among young adults. Selected male students with strong academic performance may be assigned up to three female participants who have either volunteered or been placed in the program due to disciplinary review. Participants receive housing, financial benefits, and long-term support incentives for successful pregnancies. Within the program’s framework, assigned participants are expected to cooperate with the designated student leader and follow the established program structure, working together under government supervision to fulfill the initiative’s reproductive and social objectives.

INITIAL MESSAGES
